Title: Overexpression of soybean flavonoid 3′-hydroxylase enhances plant salt tolerance by promoting ascorbic acid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: COP9 signalosome subunit 5B; MPN (Mpr1-Pad1 N-terminal) domain-containing protein
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmCSN5B physically interacts with GmF3′H and GmVTC1. 2. GmF3′H binds the intact MPN domain of GmCSN5B. 3. GmF3′H competitively suppresses the GmCSN5B–GmVTC1 interaction, consistent with reduced VTC1 degradation and increased AsA accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative factor on AsA accumulation through interaction with VTC1; its binding by GmF3′H is associated with increased AsA biosynthesis and salt tolerance.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant for ROS scavenging, maintenance of redox homeostasis, salt stress tolerance, and essential human nutrient (vitamin C)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation assay; subcellular colocalization; competition LUC assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers CSN5B mediates VTC1 degradation based on prior work and competition assays rather than direct degradation biochemistry in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indirect engineering target in the F3′H–CSN5B–VTC1 module; weakening CSN5B interaction with VTC1 may help elevate AsA and salt tolerance, but broader COP9 functions suggest caution.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from SoyMD used for pathway-expression context; accession NA

Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmCSN5B; GmF3′H indirectly through competition for CSN5B
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmVTC1 interacts with GmCSN5B in soybean. 2. GmF3′H competitively inhibits the GmVTC1–GmCSN5B interaction. 3. This supports stabilization of GmVTC1 and enhanced AsA biosynthesis under salt stress.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive contributor to AsA biosynthesis; protection from CSN5B interaction is associated with increased AsA and improved salt tolerance.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant for ROS scavenging, maintenance of redox homeostasis, salt stress tolerance, and essential human nutrient (vitamin C)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Luciferase complementation assay; subcellular colocalization; competition LUC assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described in the paper as the rate-limiting enzyme in the L-galactose pathway of AsA biosynthesis, citing prior studies.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful companion target with F3′H to increase AsA and salt tolerance through stabilization of the AsA biosynthetic enzyme.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: COP9 signalosome subunit 5B; MPN domain-containing protein
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCSN5B interacts with AtVTC1 and AtF3′H in conserved assays. 2. AtF3′H competitively interferes with AtCSN5B–AtVTC1 binding, paralleling soybean results. 3. This conserved module supports increased AsA accumulation and salt tolerance in Arabidopsis.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator context for AsA accumulation through VTC1 interaction; competition by F3′H is linked to increased AsA and salt tolerance.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant for ROS scavenging, maintenance of redox homeostasis, salt stress tolerance, and essential human nutrient (vitamin C)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Luciferase complementation assay; competition LUC assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly to demonstrate evolutionary conservation of the F3′H–CSN5B–VTC1 regulatory module.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports transferability of the module across species for metabolic engineering of salt tolerance.

Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtCSN5B; AtF3′H indirectly through competition for CSN5B
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtVTC1 interacts with AtCSN5B. 2. AtF3′H competitively weakens AtVTC1–AtCSN5B interaction. 3. The conserved competition mechanism supports increased AsA accumulation in Arabidopsis expressing GmF3′H.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive contributor to AsA biosynthesis in the conserved Arabidopsis module linked to salt tolerance.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant for ROS scavenging, maintenance of redox homeostasis, salt stress tolerance, and essential human nutrient (vitamin C)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Luciferase complementation assay; competition LUC assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included to support conservation of the mechanism; not directly genetically manipulated in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests VTC1 stabilization is a conserved lever to raise AsA in crop engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthesis; indirectly promotes L-galactose pathway of ascorbate biosynthesis via VTC1 stabilization
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 3′-hydroxylated flavonoid branch leading to dihydroxylated flavonoids/anthocyanin-proanthocyanidin related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3′-hydroxylation of flavonoid substrates to generate 3′-hydroxylated/dihydroxylated flavonoid compounds.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms flavonoid branch products and indirectly promotes ascorbic acid accumulation by stabilizing VTC1 through competition with CSN5B
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch enzyme in flavonoid metabolism; not established as rate-limiting for AsA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Soybean seed coat for flavonoids; soybean seed coat and leaves for AsA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids and AsA act as antioxidants, scavenging ROS and enhancing salt tolerance; AsA also serves as vitamin C for human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases seed germination and seedling survival under salt stress, elevates seed-coat flavonoids and tissue AsA, and reduces H2O2 and MDA; knockout or natural loss-of-function reduces salt tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in soybean seed coat; low in leaves and roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High expression in soybean seed coat; overexpression in leaves/seedlings correlates with increased AsA
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmCSN5B; self-interaction; cross-species interaction with AtCSN5B reported for conserved F3′H proteins
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress; 100-150 mM NaCl in soybean, 50-125 mM NaCl in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression in soybean and Arabidopsis; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling (flavonoids, AsA); subcellular localization; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation and competition assays; physiological assays (germination, survival, H2O2, MDA)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Seed coat pigmentation change/darker color due to elevated flavonoids; unpublished suggestion of involvement in seed storage tolerance; no major seedling flavonoid increase observed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for breeding or engineering broad-spectrum salt tolerance and nutritional enhancement; can be edited or overexpressed, including promoter engineering and visual selection through seed coat pigmentation; may be stacked with AsA-pathway targets such as VTC1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study concludes stage-specific outputs: seed salt tolerance mainly via flavonoids in seed coat, seedling salt tolerance mainly via AsA. GmF3′H is presented as an evolutionarily conserved determinant of this dual mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: SoyMD RNA-seq used for expression context; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; tt7 mutant; GmF3′H-OE lines OE-5/OE-13/OE-14
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthesis; indirectly linked to ascorbate biosynthesis through conserved CSN5B-VTC1 competition model
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3′-hydroxylation of flavonoid substrates in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms flavonoid branch products and indirectly supports AsA accumulation via conserved interaction module
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant-mediated ROS scavenging and salt tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: tt7 loss-of-function reduces seed salt tolerance; conserved F3′H function supports enhanced salt tolerance and AsA accumulation in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtCSN5B; self-interaction/cross-interaction of conserved F3′H proteins reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping (tt7); luciferase complementation and competition assays for conserved interactions; heterologous GmF3′H overexpression in Arabidopsis with AsA and stress phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for GmF3′H transgene in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports cross-species utility of F3′H-centered engineering for salt tolerance and antioxidant enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtF3′H itself was not overexpressed here; evidence comes from tt7 mutant assays and conserved interaction tests.
